source_record.title: Developing SEMA4A-directed CAR T cells to overcome low BCMA antigen density in multiple myeloma.
source_record.study_context: Translational and preclinical target exploration after BCMA CAR T relapse with low BCMA antigen density; PubMed abstract and metadata used for this v0 public extraction.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source frames relapse with low-to-negative BCMA expression after BCMA CAR T therapy as a reason to investigate additional targets.
limitations: Does not estimate all low-BCMA relapse frequency from the public abstract alone.
Low target density is not the only possible relapse mechanism.
Clinical applicability requires trial and safety evidence.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports quantitative antigen-density profiling showing high SEMA4A molecules per cell where BCMA density is low in a post-BCMA CAR T relapse cohort.
limitations: Candidate target density does not establish therapeutic efficacy.
Requires full-methods review for cohort composition and assay details.
Target expression in tumor cells must be weighed against normal-tissue safety.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports generation and screening of SEMA4A-directed CAR T constructs with preclinical activity against multiple myeloma models, including BCMA-low contexts.
limitations: Preclinical activity does not establish clinical benefit.
Phase 1 clinical readiness claims require trial registry and protocol review.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source highlights measurement fields needed for alternate-target work, including antigen density, normal-tissue reactivity, model context, and planned clinical translation.
limitations: Safety interpretation requires full preclinical package review and clinical protocol review.
Public abstract does not provide a full safety dataset.

what_this_supports: Low BCMA antigen density belongs in the BCMA antigen escape extraction field set.
Alternate-target strategies should capture target density, normal-tissue reactivity, model context, and clinical translation status.
SEMA4A can be tracked as a candidate alternate-target research signal in post-BCMA relapse maps.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not prove SEMA4A-directed CAR T therapy works in patients.
It does not recommend a SEMA4A-directed clinical trial.
It does not establish that low BCMA density is present in all post-CAR T relapse.
It does not replace safety, eligibility, or regulatory review.

review_notes: Built from PubMed ESummary and EFetch metadata/abstract for PMID 41072416.
Tracked as translational and preclinical signal extraction, not a clinical claim.
